summary: Enhancement 10 (Health & Wellbeing — Medical Documents CRUD) tested end-to-end. Backend: 17/17 pytest tests PASSED in 2.91s covering /types, list, list filter, upload happy-path, upload validation (invalid doc_type 400, empty file 400), get, download (base64 round-trip verified by decoding), update + GET-verify, update invalid doc_type 400, update 404, delete + GET 404, delete 404. Regression smoke for /api/health/medications, /api/health/appointments, /api/health/insurance all 200. Verified upload response strips content_b64, user_id and _id. Frontend: logged in as test1@plos.app, navigated to /health, scrolled to medical-docs-card. doc-upload button visible; clicking opens browser file chooser (expo-document-picker renders <input type=file> on web) → uploaded a text file → new row appears immediately with title, type label ("Other"), and size. Tapped row → doc-editor modal opens with Title input, 10 Document-Type pills (Lab result/Imaging/Prescription/Discharge summary/Vaccine record/Insurance card/Visit note/Specialist referral/Test report/Other), Date input, Provider/Doctor input, Notes textarea, Delete & Save buttons (modal has 4 inputs as expected). Edited title → Save → modal closed and row text updated to new title (verified textually). Re-opened row → tapped Delete → inline confirm-box appeared (testID doc-editor-confirm) → tapped doc-editor-confirm-delete → row removed from list. Smoke after: med-add, appt-add, insurance-card, medical-docs-card, doc-upload all present. 0 console errors. All TEST_UI/playwright leftover docs cleaned up. No deprecated Expo packages or RN issues spotted in this module.
critical_code_review_comments: medical_docs.py: clean and idempotent. Pops _id and user_id before returning, also strips content_b64 from list/get to avoid huge payloads. Validates doc_type against ALLOWED_DOC_TYPES on upload, update, and list filter. Enforces 15 MB max and rejects empty files with 400. Auto-infers mime from extension when client sends application/octet-stream. UUID doc_id avoids ObjectId leakage entirely.

Frontend health/index.tsx: medical-docs-card and doc-editor wired correctly. pickAndUploadDoc handles web (uses asset.file) vs native (fetch+blob) branches. doc-{id} rows render title, docTypeLabel, doc_date, provider, and human-readable size. Edit modal reuses EditModal so confirm-delete pattern is consistent with other modules.
medicalDocsApi.upload builds FormData with proper Authorization Bearer token from getToken() and uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL — no hardcoded URLs.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — upload, edit, delete, smoke checks all verified; 0 console errors

seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data. Created TEST_-prefixed docs during pytest (cleaned via DELETE fixture teardown). Uploaded playwright_upload.txt and playwright_edit_doc.txt during UI run and deleted them via the UI's own delete flow at end of run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Enhancement 10 backend endpoints and UI flows pass. If re-testing on web: expo-document-picker on web renders an <input type=file>, so use page.expect_file_chooser() then fc.set_files(path) after clicking [data-testid="doc-upload"]. The EditModal delete is a two-step inline confirm — click 'Delete' first, then click [data-testid="doc-editor-confirm-delete"] (NOT a browser dialog, no page.on('dialog') needed).
